institutions.institution_blurb: Partner in the Hartford mercantile firm Hooker & Chaffee.

INST070.name: Hooker and Chaffee
INST070.notes:
One of the largest and most flourishing mercantile houses of New England before the Revolutionary War. Partners: James Hooker (H00348, silent partner), Horace Hooker (H00350, his brother), and John Chaffee (brother of James's third wife Mary Chaffee). Transacted much business for the Colonial Government. All partners were devoted patriots who gave freely of their means for the Colonial cause. After the war, serious embarrassment from heavy French and Spanish spoliations caused failure and dissolution, scattering its members. For full account see Stiles's New History of Windsor. SUPPLY CHAIN NOTE: The firm operated alongside Jeremiah Wadsworth's commissary operation — both Hooker descendants and Talcott descendants provisioning the Continental Army from the same Connecticut River Valley network. See also LM015 (James Hooker House, 118 Palisado Avenue, Windsor).
[former/alt detail] Hooker & Chaffee; Hooker & Chafee
